title: Route descending sort puts unknown routes first
reasoning: The route comparator maps null chain IDs to Infinity, which sinks unknown routes for ascending order. The descending branch reverses the subtraction, so a null sourceChainId compares as Infinity and sorts ahead of known routes. This contradicts the module's no-data-sinks UX pattern used by nullable columns and specifically creates a descending route sort where unknown route rows occupy the top of the table.
reproduction: Create one transfer with sourceChainId/destChainId null and one with known chain IDs, then call sortTransfers([known, unknown], "route", "desc", rates). The unknown transfer sorts first.
recommendation: Handle missing route parts explicitly before applying direction, or reuse compareNullable over a tuple-like route value so null routes sink for both asc and desc.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The route tests only assert null sinking for ascending order; there is no descending route test with null sourceChainId or destChainId.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a sortTransfers route test asserting unknown chain IDs sink on descending as well as ascending.
minimumFixScope: Update compareRoute in ui-dashboard/src/lib/bridge-flows/sort.ts and add the missing descending null-route regression test.

history.note: Fixed route sort so unknown chain IDs sink for both directions and added descending null-route coverage.
